figures.caption: Table 4: Visual predicate classifier hyperparameters. The output dimension is the selected predicate dimension m from Table 1.

Figure 2: Learning the image-grounded task graph: A student encoder maps the current observation to a quantized FSQ code, while an EMA teacher encoder provides the target successor code. An action-conditioned dynamics model predicts the next code from the current code and action identifier, and an inverse model predicts the action from current and successor codes. Unique quantized codes define visual task-graph nodes, and observed transitions define labeled graph edges.
Table 2: Image-grounded task-graph training hyperparameters. We swept the FSQ latent dimensionality for each domain and selected the final checkpoint using training loss and latent-graph diagnostics.
Figure 5: Negative-evidence ablation on DinnerTable and DinnerTable Real. Removing negative evidence makes planning fail, even when the solver is guided with the true minimum number of task states.
